publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-12-16 18:17:57 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes correspondence, course material, publications, and other records relating to Jirousek's career and her interest in the textiles and dress of Turkey.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Jirousek earned a B.A. in sociology from Hamline University in 1960, an M.A. in applied design in 1982 and a PhD in design, housing and apparel in 1988, both at the University of Minnesota. She was an assistant professor and curator at the University of Alabama from 1988-1992 and from 1992-2014 was the associate professor of textiles and apparel and curator of the costume collection in the College of Human Ecology at Cornell.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, course material, publications, and other records relating to Jirousek's career and her interest in the textiles and dress of Turkey.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Charlotte Jirousek papers, #23-19-4123. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also collections #47-10-3394 and #23-19-2807 for additional biographical information.

controlaccess.subject: Textile fabrics.
Clothing and dress -- Turkey.

Costume -- History.
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Textiles and Apparel
New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design

did.unittitle: Master's thesis - "Pattern, Form and Color in Weaving"
"Pattern, Form and Color in Weaving" Plan B paper by Jirousek for M.A. degree - University of Minnesota
PhD thesis from the University of Minnesota "Applied Design in a Cross Cultural Context: Designing for Handicrafts in Jamaican Development"
"More Than Oriental Splendor: Western Dress and the Levant"
"The End of the Silk Road: The Decline of Silk Production in Bursa in the 20th Century"
"Dyes in Rugs from the Milas Area"
"Designing in a Cross Cultural Context: Handicrafts in Jamaica"
"Indigenous Textile Craft Materials in Jamaica: Survivals of Early Dye and Straw Work Industries"
"Quilts: A New Look at an Old Subject" - Hennepin County History
"Establishing Policies and Procedures for the Organization of a Historic Costume Collection in the University Setting"
"Superstars, Superheroes and the Male Body Image: The Visual Implications of Football Uniforms"
"Market Effects on the Design and Construction of Carpets in the Milas Region of Southwestern Turkey, 1963-1993"
"More Than Oriental Splendor: European Ottoman Headgear, 1380-1580"
"Dress as Social Policy: Change in Women's Dress in a Southwestern Turkish Village"
"From Traditional to Mass Fashion System Dress Among Men in a Turkish Village"
"Ottoman Influence in Balkan Dress" - Chapter two
"The Art of the Needle" exhibit notes
"Income Generation for Rural Women" - Workshop in Jamaica sponsored by the American Home Economics Association
"The Carpets of Comlekci: The Process of Carpet Weaving in a Turkish Village: Film Proposal
Subject files and notes concerning various projects, classes taught, and publications
Art history papers, files on Turkey, personal calendars, and notebooks
ITAA annual meeting - Abstract for poster session
Articles and label copy on pile carpets
Memos, handouts, patterns, publications, correspondence concerning color studies and weaving
Engravings, etchings, postcards from Turkey and surrounding areas
History of costume course - University of Alabama

ARIT grant "Survivals of Traditional Textile Technologies in Turkey

Video taken of weaving, Karagoz, Agastya for research purposes
Video taken of shops, looms, schools in Tire, Comlekci and Yesilyurt for research purposes
